title: Reversible passwords found in GPOs
remediation:
In order to remove the preferences that contain passwords data, use Group Policy Management Console (GPMC) on a domain controller or from a client that has Remote Server Administration Tools (RSAT) installed. You can remove any preference following these steps:
1.In GPMC, open the Group Policy reported in the Exposed entities tab.
2.Navigate to the preference configuration that contains password data and delete the object. Click Apply and OK to save your changes.
3.Wait a Group Policy refresh cycle to allow changes to propagate to clients (usually up to 120 minutes)
4.After changes are applied to all clients, delete the preference.
5.Repeat steps 1 through 5 as needed to clean your whole environment.
For more information, please refer to the security advisory in the "Learn More" section.
remediationImpact: A user, scheduled task, service or application that relies on these credentials may stop functioning.
